Implement the following plan:

# UI Enhancement Plan: Typography, Visual Hierarchy & Delight

## Context

The Grock mobile app (React Native, Android-only) has a solid warm color palette and animation system, but the UI feels flat and generic due to: system-only fonts with no typographic character, every card looking identical (same radius/border/padding/bg), and screens like Menu/History using raw inline styles without the theme system. This plan enhances typography with custom fonts, creates visual depth through card differentiation, and polishes details across all screens.

---

## 1. Custom Fonts: Nunito + Source Sans 3

**Why these fonts:** Nunito's rounded terminals match the warm, friendly grocery-app character and harmonize with the rounded UI (20px border-radius). Source Sans 3 provides clean contrast for body text — humanist and legible at small sizes.

### 1a. Download & install fonts

Create `android/app/src/main/assets/fonts/` with 6 TTF files:
- `Nunito-SemiBold.ttf` (600), `Nunito-Bold.ttf` (700), `Nunito-ExtraBold.ttf` (800)
- `SourceSans3-Regular.ttf` (400), `SourceSans3-SemiBold.ttf` (600), `SourceSans3-Bold.ttf` (700)

Use a download script (`scripts/download-fonts.sh`) that fetches from Google Fonts API.

### 1b. Create `react-native.config.js` (project root)

```js
module.exports = {
  project: { android: {} },
  assets: ['./android/app/src/main/assets/fonts/'],
};
```

> On RN 0.84 with New Architecture, TTFs in `assets/fonts/` are auto-bundled. No linking step needed.

---

## 2. Theme Token Updates

### `src/theme/typography.ts` — Full rewrite

Replace `fontWeight` with `fontFamily` (Android resolves weight from filename, not fontWeight prop with custom fonts).

| Token | Font | Size | Letter Spacing | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `headline` | Nunito-ExtraBold | 32 | -0.5 | Hero text, greetings |
| `title` | Nunito-Bold | 22 | -0.3 | Card/section titles |
| **`subtitle`** (new) | Nunito-SemiBold | 18 | -0.2 | Secondary headings |
| `body` | SourceSans3-Regular | 16 | — | Descriptions |
| `caption` | SourceSans3-Regular | 13 | — | Metadata, dates |
| `label` | SourceSans3-SemiBold | 14 | — | Chip labels, prices |
| **`eyebrow`** (new) | SourceSans3-Bold | 12 | 1.2 | Uppercase overlines |
| **`numericDisplay`** (new) | Nunito-Bold | 24 | -0.5 | Stat values, totals |
| **`button`** (new) | SourceSans3-Bold | 15 | 0.3 | Button text |

### `src/theme/colors.ts` — Add 4 new tokens (no existing changes)

- `surfaceElevated: '#fffcf7'` — warm-tinted white for prominent cards
- `subtleInk: '#a39a8e'` — tertiary text (dates, struck items)
- `divider: '#f2e8d8'` — softer than `line` for item separators
- `brandWash: '#fff5ed'` — faint brand tint for hero backgrounds

### `src/theme/spacing.ts` — Add 2 tokens

- `section: 40` — generous section spacing
- `page: 20` — screen edge padding

### `src/theme/elevation.ts` — Bump values, add `level4`

Increase existing levels slightly (e.g., level1 elevation 1->2, level3 elevation 6->8). Add `level4` (elevation 12) for tab bar and bottom sheets.

### `src/theme/motion.ts` — Add entrance preset + stagger helper

- `entrance` spring: damping 18, stiffness 160, mass 0.9
- `stagger(index, baseDelay=50)` helper capped at 300ms

---

## 3. Shared Component Changes

### `src/components/Card.tsx`
Add `variant` prop: `'default' | 'outlined' | 'filled' | 'elevated'`
- **default**: surface bg + line border (current behavior)
- **outlined**: transparent bg + 1.5px line border
- **filled**: surfaceAlt bg + no border (for inset data)
- **elevated**: surfaceElevated bg + no border (for hero cards)
- Reduce borderRadius 24 -> 20

### `src/components/SectionHeader.tsx`
- Use `typography.subtitle` for title (was label+16px)
- Branded badge: `brandLight` bg, `brandDeep` text (was surfaceAlt/mutedInk)
- Add 3px left `brand` accent stripe

### `src/components/Chip.tsx`
- Apply `fontFamily: 'SourceSans3-SemiBold'` (replace fontWeight)

### `src/components/SearchBar.tsx`
- Apply `fontFamily: 'SourceSans3-Regular'` to input

### `src/components/EmptyState.tsx`
- Increase padding 32->48, add maxWidth 260 on message

### `src/components/FloatingActionBar.tsx`
- Use `surfaceElevated` bg, remove border, use `elevation.level4`

### `src/components/AnimatedPressable.tsx`
- Deepen press: scale 0.97->0.965, opacity 0.85->0.8

### `src/components/navigation/GrockTabBar.tsx`
- Height 80->72, remove top border, use `surfaceElevated` bg + `level4` elevation
- Font: `SourceSans3-SemiBold` inactive / `SourceSans3-Bold` active, 11px

### `src/components/navigation/TabBarIndicatorPill.tsx`
- Refine: width 64->56, height 32->28, borderRadius 16->14

---

## 4. Screen-Level Changes

### Home Screen
| File | Key Changes |
|---|---|
| `HomeHeader.tsx` | Use `typography.eyebrow` for "GROCK", `typography.headline` for greeting (now Nunito-ExtraBold) |
| `StatCard.tsx` | Remove border, use `typography.eyebrow` for label + `typography.numericDisplay` at 20px for value. Switch entrance from `withTiming` to `withSpring(motion.entrance)` |
| `ActiveCartCard.tsx` | Use `surfaceElevated` bg, remove border, add `elevation.level2`. Name uses `typography.subtitle` |
| `CompletedCartCard.tsx` | Keep surface bg, use softer `divider` border. Dates use `subtleInk` |
| `QuickActionButton.tsx` | Remove border, add `elevation.level1`. Label uses `typography.label` |
| `InsightsPanel.tsx` | Stat values use `typography.numericDisplay` at 16px |
| `HomeScreen.tsx` | Add staggered `FadeInUp` entrance on stat cards using `stagger()`. Increase header gap 18->22 |

### Cart Screens
| File | Key Changes |
|---|---|
| `CartHero.tsx` | Name uses `typography.headline` at 28px. Badge uses `typography.eyebrow` |
| `BudgetBar.tsx` | Wrap in surfaceAlt container with 16px borderRadius. Spent uses `numericDisplay` at 20px. Track height 8->10 |
| `CartItemRow.tsx` | Item name uses `SourceSans3-SemiBold`. Struck items use `subtleInk`. Mark button gets faint success wash bg |
| `CartSummaryBar.tsx` | Total uses `numericDisplay` at 20px |
| `CartStatusButton.tsx` | Text uses `typography.button`. Add `elevation.level1` |
| `CartListScreen.tsx` | CartCard: `surfaceElevated` bg, no border, `elevation.level2`. Name uses `subtitle`. Status badge uses `eyebrow`. Quick create inputs get `SourceSans3-Regular` font, button uses `typography.button` |

### Vault Screen
| File | Key Changes |
|---|---|
| `VaultItemCard.tsx` | Name uses `SourceSans3-SemiBold`. Price uses `subtleInk` |
| `AddItemSheet.tsx` | `surfaceElevated` bg, no border, `elevation.level4`, borderRadius 24->28. Inputs get `SourceSans3-Regular`. Button uses `typography.button` |
| `AddCategorySheet.tsx` | Same treatment as AddItemSheet |

### Menu & History Screens
| File | Key Changes |
|---|---|
| `MenuScreen.tsx` | Title uses `typography.headline`. Card titles use `typography.subtitle`. Body uses `typography.body`. Buttons: swap `Pressable` -> `AnimatedPressable` for spring press feedback. Button text uses `typography.button`. Inputs get font family. Use `divider` border color |
| `HistoryScreen.tsx` | Same typography migration. Totals use `numericDisplay` at 20px. Add staggered `FadeInUp` entrance on cards via `stagger()`. Use `subtleInk` for dates |

### Separator refinement (VaultScreen + CartDetailScreen)
- Height: 1px -> `StyleSheet.hairlineWidth`
- Color: `line` -> `divider`
- Margin: 18 -> 20

---

## 5. Implementation Order

**Batch 1 — Foundation (no visual changes until batch 2):**
1. Download fonts + create `android/app/src/main/assets/fonts/`
2. Create `react-native.config.js`
3. Update `typography.ts`, `colors.ts`, `spacing.ts`, `elevation.ts`, `motion.ts`

**Batch 2 — Shared components (high leverage):**
4. `Card.tsx` (variant system)
5. `SectionHeader.tsx`, `Chip.tsx`, `SearchBar.tsx`, `EmptyState.tsx`
6. `FloatingActionBar.tsx`, `AnimatedPressable.tsx`
7. Tab bar: `GrockTabBar.tsx`, `TabBarIndicatorPill.tsx`

**Batch 3 — Home screen:**
8. `HomeHeader.tsx`, `StatCard.tsx`, `ActiveCartCard.tsx`, `CompletedCartCard.tsx`
9. `QuickActionButton.tsx`, `InsightsPanel.tsx`, `HomeScreen.tsx`

**Batch 4 — Cart screens:**
10. `CartHero.tsx`, `BudgetBar.tsx`, `CartItemRow.tsx`
11. `CartSummaryBar.tsx`, `CartStatusButton.tsx`, `CartListScreen.tsx`, `CartDetailScreen.tsx`

**Batch 5 — Vault + remaining:**
12. `VaultItemCard.tsx`, `AddItemSheet.tsx`, `AddCategorySheet.tsx`, `VaultScreen.tsx`
13. `MenuScreen.tsx`, `HistoryScreen.tsx`

---

## 6. Verification

1. **Font rendering:** After batch 1, add a temporary `<Text style={{fontFamily: 'Nunito-ExtraBold', fontSize: 32}}>Test</Text>` in App.tsx to confirm fonts load
2. **Clean Android build:** `cd android && ./gradlew clean && cd .. && npx react-native run-android`
3. **Visual check per screen:** Navigate through Home -> Vault -> Cart -> Menu -> History after each batch
4. **Card variants:** Confirm elevated cards have visible shadow lift vs filled/outlined
5. **Animations:** Verify staggered entrances on Home stat cards and History cards
6. **Tab bar:** Confirm reduced height, removed border, pill indicator alignment
7. **Typography hierarchy:** Headlines (Nunito-ExtraBold) should feel distinctly different from body (SourceSans3-Regular)

---

## Files Modified (37 total)

**New files (3):**
- `android/app/src/main/assets/fonts/` (6 TTF files)
- `react-native.config.js`
- `scripts/download-fonts.sh`

**Theme (5):** `typography.ts`, `colors.ts`, `spacing.ts`, `elevation.ts`, `motion.ts`

**Shared components (8):** `Card.tsx`, `SectionHeader.tsx`, `Chip.tsx`, `SearchBar.tsx`, `EmptyState.tsx`, `FloatingActionBar.tsx`, `AnimatedPressable.tsx`, `GrockTabBar.tsx`, `TabBarIndicatorPill.tsx`

**Home (7):** `HomeHeader.tsx`, `StatCard.tsx`, `ActiveCartCard.tsx`, `CompletedCartCard.tsx`, `QuickActionButton.tsx`, `InsightsPanel.tsx`, `HomeScreen.tsx`

**Cart (7):** `CartHero.tsx`, `BudgetBar.tsx`, `CartItemRow.tsx`, `CartSummaryBar.tsx`, `CartStatusButton.tsx`, `CartListScreen.tsx`, `CartDetailScreen.tsx`

**Vault (4):** `VaultItemCard.tsx`, `AddItemSheet.tsx`, `AddCategorySheet.tsx`, `VaultScreen.tsx`

**Other (2):** `MenuScreen.tsx`, `HistoryScreen.tsx`

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Downloads Nunito and Source Sans 3 font files from Google Fonts.
# Usage: bash scripts/download-fonts.sh

set -euo pipefail

FONT_DIR="android/app/src/main/assets/fonts"
mkdir -p "$FONT_DIR"

BASE="https://github.com/google/fonts/raw/main"

declare -A FONTS=(
  ["Nunito-SemiBold.ttf"]="$BASE/google/nunito/Nunito-SemiBold.ttf"
  ["Nunito-Bold.ttf"]="$BASE/google/nunito/Nunito-Bold.ttf"
  ["Nunito-ExtraBold.ttf"]="$BASE/google/nunito/Nunito-ExtraBold.ttf"
  ["SourceSans3-Regular.ttf"]="$BASE/google/sourcesans3/SourceSans3-Regular.ttf"
  ["SourceSans3-SemiBold.ttf"]="$BASE/google/sourcesans3/SourceSans3-SemiBold.ttf"
  ["SourceSans3-Bold.ttf"]="$BASE/google/sourcesans3/SourceSans3-Bold.ttf"
)

for file in "${!FONTS[@]}"; do
  url="${FONTS[$file]}"
  dest="$FONT_DIR/$file"
  if [ -f "$dest" ]; then
    echo "Skipping $file (already exists)"
  else
    echo "Downloading $file..."
    curl -fSL --retry 3 -o "$dest" "$url"
  fi
done

echo "Done. Fonts saved to $FONT_DIR/"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Downloads Nunito and Source Sans 3 font files from Google Fonts.
# Usage: bash scripts/download-fonts.sh

set -euo pipefail

FONT_DIR="android/app/src/main/assets/fonts"
mkdir -p "$FONT_DIR"

BASE="https://github.com/google/fonts/raw/main"

declare -A FONTS=(
  ["Nunito-SemiBold.ttf"]="$BASE/google/nunito/Nunito-SemiBold.ttf"
  ["Nunito-Bold.ttf"]="$BASE/google/nunito/Nunito-Bold.ttf"
  ["Nunito-ExtraBold.ttf"]="$BASE/google/nunito/Nunito-ExtraBold.ttf"
  ["SourceSans3-Regular.ttf"]="$BASE/google/sourcesans3/SourceSans3-Regular.ttf"
  ["SourceSans3-SemiBold.ttf"]="$BASE/google/sourcesans3/SourceSans3-SemiBold.ttf"
  ["SourceSans3-Bold.ttf"]="$BASE/google/sourcesans3/SourceSans3-Bold.ttf"
)

for file in "${!FONTS[@]}"; do
  url="${FONTS[$file]}"
  dest="$FONT_DIR/$file"
  if [ -f "$dest" ]; then
    echo "Skipping $file (already exists)"
  else
    echo "Downloading $file..."
    curl -fSL --retry 3 -o "$dest" "$url"
  fi
done

echo "Done. Fonts saved to $FONT_DIR/"

# UI Enhancement Plan: Typography, Visual Hierarchy & Delight

## Context

The Grock mobile app (React Native, Android-only) has a solid warm color palette and animation system, but the UI feels flat and generic due to: system-only fonts with no typographic character, every card looking identical (same radius/border/padding/bg), and screens like Menu/History using raw inline styles without the theme system. This plan enhances typography with custom fonts, creates visual depth through card differentiation, and polishes details across all screens.

---

## 1. Custom Fonts: Nunito + Source Sans 3

**Why these fonts:** Nunito's rounded terminals match the warm, friendly grocery-app character and harmonize with the rounded UI (20px border-radius). Source Sans 3 provides clean contrast for body text — humanist and legible at small sizes.

### 1a. Download & install fonts

Create `android/app/src/main/assets/fonts/` with 6 TTF files:
- `Nunito-SemiBold.ttf` (600), `Nunito-Bold.ttf` (700), `Nunito-ExtraBold.ttf` (800)
- `SourceSans3-Regular.ttf` (400), `SourceSans3-SemiBold.ttf` (600), `SourceSans3-Bold.ttf` (700)

Use a download script (`scripts/download-fonts.sh`) that fetches from Google Fonts API.

### 1b. Create `react-native.config.js` (project root)

```js
module.exports = {
  project: { android: {} },
  assets: ['./android/app/src/main/assets/fonts/'],
};
```

> On RN 0.84 with New Architecture, TTFs in `assets/fonts/` are auto-bundled. No linking step needed.

---

## 2. Theme Token Updates

### `src/theme/typography.ts` — Full rewrite

Replace `fontWeight` with `fontFamily` (Android resolves weight from filename, not fontWeight prop with custom fonts).

| Token | Font | Size | Letter Spacing | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `headline` | Nunito-ExtraBold | 32 | -0.5 | Hero text, greetings |
| `title` | Nunito-Bold | 22 | -0.3 | Card/section titles |
| **`subtitle`** (new) | Nunito-SemiBold | 18 | -0.2 | Secondary headings |
| `body` | SourceSans3-Regular | 16 | — | Descriptions |
| `caption` | SourceSans3-Regular | 13 | — | Metadata, dates |
| `label` | SourceSans3-SemiBold | 14 | — | Chip labels, prices |
| **`eyebrow`** (new) | SourceSans3-Bold | 12 | 1.2 | Uppercase overlines |
| **`numericDisplay`** (new) | Nunito-Bold | 24 | -0.5 | Stat values, totals |
| **`button`** (new) | SourceSans3-Bold | 15 | 0.3 | Button text |

### `src/theme/colors.ts` — Add 4 new tokens (no existing changes)

- `surfaceElevated: '#fffcf7'` — warm-tinted white for prominent cards
- `subtleInk: '#a39a8e'` — tertiary text (dates, struck items)
- `divider: '#f2e8d8'` — softer than `line` for item separators
- `brandWash: '#fff5ed'` — faint brand tint for hero backgrounds

### `src/theme/spacing.ts` — Add 2 tokens

- `section: 40` — generous section spacing
- `page: 20` — screen edge padding

### `src/theme/elevation.ts` — Bump values, add `level4`

Increase existing levels slightly (e.g., level1 elevation 1->2, level3 elevation 6->8). Add `level4` (elevation 12) for tab bar and bottom sheets.

### `src/theme/motion.ts` — Add entrance preset + stagger helper

- `entrance` spring: damping 18, stiffness 160, mass 0.9
- `stagger(index, baseDelay=50)` helper capped at 300ms

---

## 3. Shared Component Changes

### `src/components/Card.tsx`

# UI Enhancement Plan: Typography, Visual Hierarchy & Delight

## Context

The Grock mobile app (React Native, Android-only) has a solid warm color palette and animation system, but the UI feels flat and generic due to: system-only fonts with no typographic character, every card looking identical (same radius/border/padding/bg), and screens like Menu/History using raw inline styles without the theme system. This plan enhances typography with custom fonts, creates visual depth through card differentiation, and polishes details across all screens.

---

## 1. Custom Fonts: Nunito + Source Sans 3

**Why these fonts:** Nunito's rounded terminals match the warm, friendly grocery-app character and harmonize with the rounded UI (20px border-radius). Source Sans 3 provides clean contrast for body text — humanist and legible at small sizes.

### 1a. Download & install fonts

Create `android/app/src/main/assets/fonts/` with 6 TTF files:
- `Nunito-SemiBold.ttf` (600), `Nunito-Bold.ttf` (700), `Nunito-ExtraBold.ttf` (800)
- `SourceSans3-Regular.ttf` (400), `SourceSans3-SemiBold.ttf` (600), `SourceSans3-Bold.ttf` (700)

Use a download script (`scripts/download-fonts.sh`) that fetches from Google Fonts API.

### 1b. Create `react-native.config.js` (project root)

```js
module.exports = {
  project: { android: {} },
  assets: ['./android/app/src/main/assets/fonts/'],
};
```

> On RN 0.84 with New Architecture, TTFs in `assets/fonts/` are auto-bundled. No linking step needed.

---

## 2. Theme Token Updates

### `src/theme/typography.ts` — Full rewrite

Replace `fontWeight` with `fontFamily` (Android resolves weight from filename, not fontWeight prop with custom fonts).

| Token | Font | Size | Letter Spacing | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `headline` | Nunito-ExtraBold | 32 | -0.5 | Hero text, greetings |
| `title` | Nunito-Bold | 22 | -0.3 | Card/section titles |
| **`subtitle`** (new) | Nunito-SemiBold | 18 | -0.2 | Secondary headings |
| `body` | SourceSans3-Regular | 16 | — | Descriptions |
| `caption` | SourceSans3-Regular | 13 | — | Metadata, dates |
| `label` | SourceSans3-SemiBold | 14 | — | Chip labels, prices |
| **`eyebrow`** (new) | SourceSans3-Bold | 12 | 1.2 | Uppercase overlines |
| **`numericDisplay`** (new) | Nunito-Bold | 24 | -0.5 | Stat values, totals |
| **`button`** (new) | SourceSans3-Bold | 15 | 0.3 | Button text |

### `src/theme/colors.ts` — Add 4 new tokens (no existing changes)

- `surfaceElevated: '#fffcf7'` — warm-tinted white for prominent cards
- `subtleInk: '#a39a8e'` — tertiary text (dates, struck items)
- `divider: '#f2e8d8'` — softer than `line` for item separators
- `brandWash: '#fff5ed'` — faint brand tint for hero backgrounds

### `src/theme/spacing.ts` — Add 2 tokens

- `section: 40` — generous section spacing
- `page: 20` — screen edge padding

### `src/theme/elevation.ts` — Bump values, add `level4`

Increase existing levels slightly (e.g., level1 elevation 1->2, level3 elevation 6->8). Add `level4` (elevation 12) for tab bar and bottom sheets.

### `src/theme/motion.ts` — Add entrance preset + stagger helper

- `entrance` spring: damping 18, stiffness 160, mass 0.9
- `stagger(index, baseDelay=50)` helper capped at 300ms

# Collapsible Categories on Vault Screen

## Context

The Vault screen displays items grouped by category using a `SectionList` with sticky headers. When the vault has many categories with many items, it's hard to quickly navigate. Adding the ability to tap a category header to collapse/expand its items will improve navigation and reduce visual clutter.

---

## Approach

Use local component state (`Set<string>` of collapsed category UIDs) in `VaultScreen`. When a category is collapsed, pass an empty `data` array to the `SectionList` for that section. The section header gets an `onPress` handler and a chevron icon that animates rotation between expanded (down) and collapsed (right).

**Why local state:** Collapse state is ephemeral UI state — it resets on screen remount, which is the expected behavior. No need to persist in Zustand or SQLite.

**Why empty `data` array (not filtering sections):** SectionList still renders the sticky header for collapsed sections, allowing the user to tap to re-expand. Removing the section entirely would hide the header.

---

## Files Modified (3)

### 1. `src/components/SectionHeader.tsx`

Add optional collapse support:

- New props: `onPress?: () => void`, `collapsed?: boolean`
- When `onPress` is provided, wrap the container in `AnimatedPressable`
- Add a chevron icon (MaterialCommunityIcons `chevron-down`) to the right side, before the count badge
- Animate chevron rotation: `0deg` when expanded, `-90deg` when collapsed, using `withSpring(motion.snappy)`
- Import `AnimatedPressable`, `Animated` from reanimated, `useAnimatedStyle`/`withSpring`/`useSharedValue`, `motion`, and `MaterialCommunityIcons`

```
Layout when onPress provided:
[stripe] [title]                [chevron ▾] [count badge]
```

The chevron uses a Reanimated shared value driven by the `collapsed` prop. Rotation transitions smoothly via `withSpring`.

### 2. `src/screens/vault/components/VaultSectionHeader.tsx`

- Add props: `onToggle: () => void`, `collapsed: boolean`
- Pass through to `SectionHeader` as `onPress={onToggle}` and `collapsed={collapsed}`

### 3. `src/screens/vault/VaultScreen.tsx`

- Add state: `const [collapsedIds, setCollapsedIds] = useState<Set<string>>(new Set())`
- Add toggle handler:
  ```ts
  const toggleCollapse = useCallback((categoryId: string) => {
    setCollapsedIds(prev => {
      const next = new Set(prev);
      if (next.has(categoryId)) next.delete(categoryId);
      else next.add(categoryId);
      return next;
    });
  }, []);
  ```
- In the `sections` useMemo, after building each section, replace `data` with `[]` if `collapsedIds.has(cat.uid)`
- Update `renderSectionHeader` to pass `onToggle` and `collapsed` to `VaultSectionHeader`
- Remove `getItemLayout` optimization (variable section heights make it inaccurate with collapsible sections)

---

## Verification

1. Tap a category header — items should collapse with the chevron rotating to point right
2. Tap again — items re-expand, chevron rotates back down
3. Collapsed state should persist while scrolling (sticky headers still visible)
4. Search filtering should still work correctly on both collapsed and expanded categories
5. Multiple categories can be collapsed independently
6. Adding an item to a collapsed category should work (the sheet doesn't depend on collapse state)

# UI Enhancement Plan: Typography, Visual Hierarchy & Delight

## Context

The Grock mobile app (React Native, Android-only) has a solid warm color palette and animation system, but the UI feels flat and generic due to: system-only fonts with no typographic character, every card looking identical (same radius/border/padding/bg), and screens like Menu/History using raw inline styles without the theme system. This plan enhances typography with custom fonts, creates visual depth through card differentiation, and polishes details across all screens.

---

## 1. Custom Fonts: Nunito + Source Sans 3

**Why these fonts:** Nunito's rounded terminals match the warm, friendly grocery-app character and harmonize with the rounded UI (20px border-radius). Source Sans 3 provides clean contrast for body text — humanist and legible at small sizes.

### 1a. Download & install fonts

Create `android/app/src/main/assets/fonts/` with 6 TTF files:
- `Nunito-SemiBold.ttf` (600), `Nunito-Bold.ttf` (700), `Nunito-ExtraBold.ttf` (800)
- `SourceSans3-Regular.ttf` (400), `SourceSans3-SemiBold.ttf` (600), `SourceSans3-Bold.ttf` (700)

Use a download script (`scripts/download-fonts.sh`) that fetches from Google Fonts API.

### 1b. Create `react-native.config.js` (project root)

```js
module.exports = {
  project: { android: {} },
  assets: ['./android/app/src/main/assets/fonts/'],
};
```

> On RN 0.84 with New Architecture, TTFs in `assets/fonts/` are auto-bundled. No linking step needed.

---

## 2. Theme Token Updates

### `src/theme/typography.ts` — Full rewrite

Replace `fontWeight` with `fontFamily` (Android resolves weight from filename, not fontWeight prop with custom fonts).

| Token | Font | Size | Letter Spacing | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `headline` | Nunito-ExtraBold | 32 | -0.5 | Hero text, greetings |
| `title` | Nunito-Bold | 22 | -0.3 | Card/section titles |
| **`subtitle`** (new) | Nunito-SemiBold | 18 | -0.2 | Secondary headings |
| `body` | SourceSans3-Regular | 16 | — | Descriptions |
| `caption` | SourceSans3-Regular | 13 | — | Metadata, dates |
| `label` | SourceSans3-SemiBold | 14 | — | Chip labels, prices |
| **`eyebrow`** (new) | SourceSans3-Bold | 12 | 1.2 | Uppercase overlines |
| **`numericDisplay`** (new) | Nunito-Bold | 24 | -0.5 | Stat values, totals |
| **`button`** (new) | SourceSans3-Bold | 15 | 0.3 | Button text |

### `src/theme/colors.ts` — Add 4 new tokens (no existing changes)

- `surfaceElevated: '#fffcf7'` — warm-tinted white for prominent cards
- `subtleInk: '#a39a8e'` — tertiary text (dates, struck items)
- `divider: '#f2e8d8'` — softer than `line` for item separators
- `brandWash: '#fff5ed'` — faint brand tint for hero backgrounds

### `src/theme/spacing.ts` — Add 2 tokens

- `section: 40` — generous section spacing
- `page: 20` — screen edge padding

### `src/theme/elevation.ts` — Bump values, add `level4`

Increase existing levels slightly (e.g., level1 elevation 1->2, level3 elevation 6->8). Add `level4` (elevation 12) for tab bar and bottom sheets.

### `src/theme/motion.ts` — Add entrance preset + stagger helper

- `entrance` spring: damping 18, stiffness 160, mass 0.9
- `stagger(index, baseDelay=50)` helper capped at 300ms

---

## 3. Shared Component Changes

### `src/components/Card.tsx`
Add `variant` prop: `'default' | 'outlined' | 'filled' | 'elevated'`
- **default**: surface bg + line border (current behavior)
- **outlined**: transparent bg + 1.5px line border
- **filled**: surfaceAlt bg + no border (for inset data)
- **elevated**: surfaceElevated bg + no border (for hero cards)
- Reduce borderRadius 24 -> 20

### `src/components/SectionHeader.tsx`
- Use `typography.subtitle` for title (was label+16px)
- Branded badge: `brandLight` bg, `brandDeep` text (was surfaceAlt/mutedInk)
- Add 3px left `brand` accent stripe

### `src/components/Chip.tsx`
- Apply `fontFamily: 'SourceSans3-SemiBold'` (replace fontWeight)

### `src/components/SearchBar.tsx`
- Apply `fontFamily: 'SourceSans3-Regular'` to input

### `src/components/EmptyState.tsx`
- Increase padding 32->48, add maxWidth 260 on message

### `src/components/FloatingActionBar.tsx`
- Use `surfaceElevated` bg, remove border, use `elevation.level4`

### `src/components/AnimatedPressable.tsx`
- Deepen press: scale 0.97->0.965, opacity 0.85->0.8

### `src/components/navigation/GrockTabBar.tsx`
- Height 80->72, remove top border, use `surfaceElevated` bg + `level4` elevation
- Font: `SourceSans3-SemiBold` inactive / `SourceSans3-Bold` active, 11px

### `src/components/navigation/TabBarIndicatorPill.tsx`
- Refine: width 64->56, height 32->28, borderRadius 16->14

---

## 4. Screen-Level Changes

### Home Screen
| File | Key Changes |
|---|---|
| `HomeHeader.tsx` | Use `typography.eyebrow` for "GROCK", `typography.headline` for greeting (now Nunito-ExtraBold) |
| `StatCard.tsx` | Remove border, use `typography.eyebrow` for label + `typography.numericDisplay` at 20px for value. Switch entrance from `withTiming` to `withSpring(motion.entrance)` |
| `ActiveCartCard.tsx` | Use `surfaceElevated` bg, remove border, add `elevation.level2`. Name uses `typography.subtitle` |
| `CompletedCartCard.tsx` | Keep surface bg, use softer `divider` border. Dates use `subtleInk` |
| `QuickActionButton.tsx` | Remove border, add `elevation.level1`. Label uses `typography.label` |
| `InsightsPanel.tsx` | Stat values use `typography.numericDisplay` at 16px |
| `HomeScreen.tsx` | Add staggered `FadeInUp` entrance on stat cards using `stagger()`. Increase header gap 18->22 |

### Cart Screens
| File | Key Changes |
|---|---|
| `CartHero.tsx` | Name uses `typography.headline` at 28px. Badge uses `typography.eyebrow` |
| `BudgetBar.tsx` | Wrap in surfaceAlt container with 16px borderRadius. Spent uses `numericDisplay` at 20px. Track height 8->10 |
| `CartItemRow.tsx` | Item name uses `SourceSans3-SemiBold`. Struck items use `subtleInk`. Mark button gets faint success wash bg |
| `CartSummaryBar.tsx` | Total uses `numericDisplay` at 20px |
| `CartStatusButton.tsx` | Text uses `typography.button`. Add `elevation.level1` |
| `CartListScreen.tsx` | CartCard: `surfaceElevated` bg, no border, `elevation.level2`. Name uses `subtitle`. Status badge uses `eyebrow`. Quick create inputs get `SourceSans3-Regular` font, button uses `typography.button` |

### Vault Screen
| File | Key Changes |
|---|---|
| `VaultItemCard.tsx` | Name uses `SourceSans3-SemiBold`. Price uses `subtleInk` |
| `AddItemSheet.tsx` | `surfaceElevated` bg, no border, `elevation.level4`, borderRadius 24->28. Inputs get `SourceSans3-Regular`. Button uses `typography.button` |
| `AddCategorySheet.tsx` | Same treatment as AddItemSheet |

### Menu & History Screens
| File | Key Changes |
|---|---|
| `MenuScreen.tsx` | Title uses `typography.headline`. Card titles use `typography.subtitle`. Body uses `typography.body`. Buttons: swap `Pressable` -> `AnimatedPressable` for spring press feedback. Button text uses `typography.button`. Inputs get font family. Use `divider` border color |
| `HistoryScreen.tsx` | Same typography migration. Totals use `numericDisplay` at 20px. Add staggered `FadeInUp` entrance on cards via `stagger()`. Use `subtleInk` for dates |

### Separator refinement (VaultScreen + CartDetailScreen)
- Height: 1px -> `StyleSheet.hairlineWidth`
- Color: `line` -> `divider`
- Margin: 18 -> 20

---

## 5. Implementation Order

**Batch 1 — Foundation (no visual changes until batch 2):**
1. Download fonts + create `android/app/src/main/assets/fonts/`
2. Create `react-native.config.js`
3. Update `typography.ts`, `colors.ts`, `spacing.ts`, `elevation.ts`, `motion.ts`

---

## 6. Verification

2. **Clean Android build:** `cd android && ./gradlew clean && cd .. && npx react-native run-android`

4. **Card variants:** Confirm elevated cards have visible shadow lift vs filled/outlined

# Collapsible Categories on Vault Screen

## Context

The Vault screen displays items grouped by category using a `SectionList` with sticky headers. When the vault has many categories with many items, it's hard to quickly navigate. Adding the ability to tap a category header to collapse/expand its items will improve navigation and reduce visual clutter.

---

## Approach

Use local component state (`Set<string>` of collapsed category UIDs) in `VaultScreen`. When a category is collapsed, pass an empty `data` array to the `SectionList` for that section. The section header gets an `onPress` handler and a chevron icon that animates rotation between expanded (down) and collapsed (right).

**Why local state:** Collapse state is ephemeral UI state — it resets on screen remount, which is the expected behavior. No need to persist in Zustand or SQLite.

**Why empty `data` array (not filtering sections):** SectionList still renders the sticky header for collapsed sections, allowing the user to tap to re-expand. Removing the section entirely would hide the header.

---

## Files Modified (3)

### 1. `src/components/SectionHeader.tsx`

Add optional collapse support:

- New props: `onPress?: () => void`, `collapsed?: boolean`
- When `onPress` is provided, wrap the container in `AnimatedPressable`
- Add a chevron icon (MaterialCommunityIcons `chevron-down`) to the right side, before the count badge
- Animate chevron rotation: `0deg` when expanded, `-90deg` when collapsed, using `withSpring(motion.snappy)`
- Import `AnimatedPressable`, `Animated` from reanimated, `useAnimatedStyle`/`withSpring`/`useSharedValue`, `motion`, and `MaterialCommunityIcons`

```
Layout when onPress provided:
[stripe] [title]                [chevron ▾] [count badge]
```

The chevron uses a Reanimated shared value driven by the `collapsed` prop. Rotation transitions smoothly via `withSpring`.

### 2. `src/screens/vault/components/VaultSectionHeader.tsx`

- Add props: `onToggle: () => void`, `collapsed: boolean`
- Pass through to `SectionHeader` as `onPress={onToggle}` and `collapsed={collapsed}`

### 3. `src/screens/vault/VaultScreen.tsx`

- Add state: `const [collapsedIds, setCollapsedIds] = useState<Set<string>>(new Set())`
- Add toggle handler:
  ```ts
  const toggleCollapse = useCallback((categoryId: string) => {
    setCollapsedIds(prev => {
      const next = new Set(prev);
      if (next.has(categoryId)) next.delete(categoryId);
      else next.add(categoryId);
      return next;
    });
  }, []);
  ```
- In the `sections` useMemo, after building each section, replace `data` with `[]` if `collapsedIds.has(cat.uid)`
- Update `renderSectionHeader` to pass `onToggle` and `collapsed` to `VaultSectionHeader`
- Remove `getItemLayout` optimization (variable section heights make it inaccurate with collapsible sections)

---

## Verification

1. Tap a category header — items should collapse with the chevron rotating to point right
2. Tap again — items re-expand, chevron rotates back down
3. Collapsed state should persist while scrolling (sticky headers still visible)
4. Search filtering should still work correctly on both collapsed and expanded categories
5. Multiple categories can be collapsed independently
6. Adding an item to a collapsed category should work (the sheet doesn't depend on collapse state)
